channel.title: ASO Daily
channel.description: Daily briefing on agentic search optimization (GEO/AEO/AI search) â curated by Veronica Romney for the Marketing Suite GM Org.

item.title: ASO Daily — Friday, May 22, 2026
item.description: Personal Intelligence inside Google AI Mode expands free to 200 countries and 98 languages, making earned brand presence in a user's Gmail a direct ranking signal. Plus: Search Engine Land formalizes Barnard's 'delegation boundary,' Surfer SEO finds ~70% of AI Overview citations never ranked top 10, and the GEO/AEO category crosses $200M raised across 24 platforms.
item.content:encoded:
Today's Lead

Personal Intelligence goes global and free — and the GEO unit of analysis is no longer the brand

Google quietly extended Personal Intelligence — the personalization layer inside AI Mode that references a user's Gmail and Photos data — to nearly 200 countries and 98 languages, and removed the subscription gate. The implication is the hinge point of the week: a brand's presence inside a single user's Gmail (receipts, newsletters, calendar invites) is now a direct ranking input for that user's AI Mode answer. GEO programs aimed at the category level still matter, but the new optimization frontier is the slice of earned media that lands in inboxes. Owned newsletters, transactional email, partner mentions in B2B inboxes — assets the SEO team has historically ignored — just became measurable GEO inventory.

Top Stories

85% of brand mentions in AI answers come from third parties — not your site. AI engines learn about brands primarily from reviews, media coverage, and forums; owned sites contribute roughly 15% of the citation signal. Pair with Surfer SEO's finding that ~70% of URLs cited in AI Overviews never ranked in the top 10 organic results, and the entity-stack thesis stops being theory.

Barnard's "delegation boundary" gets the long-form treatment. Search Engine Land published a feature this week formalizing the framing: the moment a user delegates a buying decision to the machine is the moment competitors get filtered out before they ever surface. The accompanying four-point diagnostic (definition, consistency, credibility, sentiment) is the entity audit GEO consultants have been quietly selling; now it has a name CMOs will repeat.

From the Operators

LinkedIn company-page feeds were unreachable this morning — Profound, Scrunch, Peec AI, AthenaHQ, Bluefish, Brandlight, Evertune AI, Gauge, Kalicube, and Masthead Media all returned shell pages with no rendered posts. Operator founders quiet overnight; nothing material captured. Will retry tomorrow with broader scrape windows.

Funding snapshot: GEO/AEO category has crossed $200M raised across 24 tracked platforms. Profound's $96M Series C at a $1B valuation, Scrunch's $15M Series A, and Brandlight's $30M raise are the largest commitments. Capital is no longer the constraint; positioning and channel are.

From the Voices

Jason Barnard (Kalicube): The delegation boundary doesn't move because you improved your site — it moves because the entity graph the machine consults moves. The four pillars (definition, consistency, credibility, sentiment) are an audit you run on the graph, not the site. Adopt the language in client conversations this week.

The empirical case is now overdetermined: Conductor's Q1 analysis of 21.9M searches shows 25.11% trigger an AI Overview. ChatGPT holds 60.7% AI-search share. 37% of consumers now start searches with AI rather than Google. 43% of marketers are actively implementing GEO strategies, up from near zero a year ago.

Tactical pushback worth pinning: Best-of listicles drive 43.8% of ChatGPT citations; a single placement seeds the brand across ChatGPT, Perplexity, and AI Overviews simultaneously. And 50% of cited content is less than 13 weeks old — the recency requirement is the operationally important number this quarter.

Industry Pulse

[Product] Personal Intelligence expands free to 200 countries and 98 languages. Earned brand presence inside Gmail becomes a direct ranking signal for personalized AI Mode answers.

[Research] Surfer SEO: ~70% of AI Overview citations never ranked top 10 organic. Entity authority, third-party signal, and structural format are now decisive — not rank.

[Funding] GEO/AEO category crosses $200M raised across 24 platforms. Capital is plentiful; the next 12 months are about agency channel and positioning.

Discussions Worth Catching

Share-of-voice as a primary metric is breaking. If 85% of citation signal comes from third parties and AI Mode personalizes against an inbox, brand-level dashboards measure the wrong unit. The KPI is share of personalized answers by audience segment that include the brand on the shortlist.

"AEO and GEO aren't separate disciplines" is procurement language. It gives CMOs cover to consolidate budget under a single program owner. Vendor positioning will compress accordingly — single-platform pitches will get more credible by end of summer.

The 13-week recency window has uncomfortable implications for evergreen content. The asset that earned the citation in February isn't earning it in May. PR and content cadence for GEO programs needs to be quarterly, not annual — and most agency retainers are not priced for that.

item.title: ASO Daily â Thursday, May 21, 2026
item.description: Google I/O 2026 reshapes AI search â Gemini 3.5 Flash is the global default for AI Mode (1B monthly users), the search box gets its first redesign in 25 years, Search Agents arrive this summer, and Universal Cart goes live with Nike, Sephora, Target, Walmart, and Wayfair. Operators move: Profound launches Agent Assistant, Scrunch expands Content Gaps, Brandlight reveals 12/12 Kimberly-Clark brands top 3 in AI engines.
item.content:encoded:
ASO Daily

Your morning briefing on agentic search optimization
Thursday, May 21, 2026 · Vol. 1 · Issue No. 4
Today's Lead

Google I/O resets the GEO board: Gemini 3.5 Flash powers AI Mode, the search box is rebuilt, and Universal Cart goes live.

Four announcements in one keynote change the brief for every GEO program. AI Mode now serves 1B monthly users on Gemini 3.5 Flash as the global default â meaning how a brand is represented in Gemini's foundational knowledge propagates across Search, the Gemini app, and every agent Google ships next. The 25-year-old search box was rebuilt around multimodal intent. Search Agents arrive this summer for AI Pro/Ultra subscribers. And the Universal Cart â Nike, Sephora, Target, Walmart, Wayfair on day one â closes the loop without the brand site. Entity consistency just stopped being a hygiene concern and became the entire surface.

Top Stories

Gemini 3.5 Flash becomes the default brain of AI Mode â globally. AI Mode queries have more than doubled every quarter since launch. Generative UI rolls out free this summer; Search Agents launch first on AI Pro/Ultra. Gemini's entity knowledge is now the upstream truth for every Google surface.

25,000 cited URLs analyzed â 63% of AI citations cluster in one format. Will Robinson's study across ChatGPT, Gemini, Copilot, Perplexity, Google AI Mode, and AI Overviews â nearly 400M responses sampled â finds 63% of citations point to a single content format. Pair with Princeton's 30â40% lift from citations/stats/quotes and the structural playbook is well-documented.

From the Operators

Profound â Agent Assistant is live. Voice-built Profound Agents that spin up in minutes. Flagship examples: a Reddit Agent that pings the brand team on competitor mentions, and a Demand Gen Agent that builds landing pages from real AI-platform queries.

Scrunch â Content Gaps gets a second act: auto-detects missing content for prompts your brand should win, then generates a brief naming the competitors and third parties filling that space.

Evertune AI â Madison Brisseaux is first out with a four-point breakdown of the I/O implications for GEO. Posted ten minutes after the keynote; fastest interpretive content of the morning.

Brandlight (Imri Marcus) â At ANA ICONS, Kimberly-Clark's Jeff Long revealed 12 of 12 Kimberly-Clark brands now rank top 3 across AI engines in under a year. Category-level proof Brandlight had not previously made public.

From the Voices

Rand Fishkin (SparkToro) â Bifurcated AI use: consumers cooling, executives accelerating with token-use bonuses. Different GEO playbooks needed for B2C vs. B2B.

Angie Perperidou â Google's new GEO guide: for Google Search, it's still SEO. Explicit "skip llms.txt and forced content chunking" is the first authoritative pushback on the tactical fringe.

Cindy Krum (MobileMoxie) â Google's own features now occupy most of mobile first-screen real estate. The metric that matters is share of visible answer space, not link rank.

Jason Barnard (Kalicube) â The "delegation boundary" reframes the visibility conversation: AI filters competitors before the user sees them. Adopt as a client diagnostic.

Industry Pulse

[Product] Universal Cart launches with Nike, Sephora, Target, Walmart, Wayfair. The "alternatives" feature is the GEO problem in one phrase.

[Research] 63% of AI citations cluster in one content format (Will Robinson, 25K URLs, 400M responses).

[Recognition] 97% of digital leaders report positive AEO impact; 94% planning increased AEO spend in 2026 (Conductor / CMS Critic).

Discussions Worth Catching

The "delegation boundary" is the cleanest sales frame of the quarter. Borrow Barnard's phrase verbatim in proposals.

Google's GEO guide draws a line in the sand. Platforms built around llms.txt now face a positioning problem.

Agency-friendly vs. agency-competing platforms. Scrunch's Content Gaps lands directly on this wedge.

ASO Daily · Curated for Veronica Romney · Tracking 10 companies, 25 individuals.

item.title: ASO Daily â Wednesday, May 20, 2026
item.description: GEO measurement gap quantified: 43% of marketers are running GEO programs in 2026, but only 14% are tracking AI citation visibility. Also: AI Mode drives 93% zero-click while brands cited in AI Overviews earn 35% more organic and 91% more paid clicks.
item.content:encoded:
ASO Daily

Your morning briefing on agentic search optimization
Wednesday, May 20, 2026Vol. 1 · Issue No. 3Curated for Veronica Romney
Today’s Lead

The GEO measurement gap is now quantified: 43% running programs, 14% measuring them

Nearly half of all marketers name GEO a core 2026 strategy, yet barely one in seven track AI citation visibility. That’s not an execution gap — it’s a governance vacuum. The platform that cracks measurement-as-a-service owns the next two years of enterprise renewals.

Top Stories

37% of consumers start product searches on AI — not Google. ChatGPT holds 60.7% of AI search market share. A brand invisible on ChatGPT is invisible to a third of the category’s consideration-stage buyers. (Search Engine Land)

AI Overviews cited brands earn 35% more organic clicks — and 91% more paid clicks. AI Mode drives 93% zero-click behavior, but brands that earn citations convert at dramatically higher rates. Brands with consistent citation AND mention are 40% more likely to resurface across consecutive queries.

Industry Pulse

[Research] Princeton confirms: content structured with citations, statistics, and quotations improves AI visibility 30–40% vs. unoptimized content.

[Product] Best-of listicles = 43.8% of ChatGPT cited page types. Pages with 3+ schema types carry 13% higher citation likelihood.

[Funding] The tracked operator cohort has collectively raised $200M+: Profound $155M ($1B valuation, 10% of Fortune 500), Bluefish $68M, Evertune $19M, Scrunch $15M, AthenaHQ $2.7M (YC).

LinkedIn unavailable this run — Chrome browser not connected. Operator/Voice sections reflect research context only.

item.title: ASO Daily â Tuesday, May 19, 2026
item.description: A Wiley-published GEO book (Ross Hudgens, Siege Media) signals mainstream category legitimacy â Lily Ray amplified 16h ago. Zero-click rates hit 83-93% with AI Overviews; Scrunch closes $15M Series A; Jason Barnard introduces the "delegation boundary" framework for brand AI confidence.
item.content:encoded:
ASO Daily

Your morning briefing on agentic search optimization

Tuesday, May 19, 2026  Â·  Vol. 1, Issue 2  Â·  Curated for Veronica Romney
TODAY'S LEAD

A mainstream publisher just greenlit a book called "GEO" â the category now has an ISBN

Ross Hudgens (CEO, Siege Media) is writing GEO for Wiley, Q4 2026. Lily Ray amplified 16h ago. When a major trade publisher assigns a book to a category, that category has crossed from practitioner niche to institutional legitimacy.

Top Stories

37% of consumers now start searches on AI, not Google â Search Engine Land behavioral study. ChatGPT at 60.7% AI search share.

Zero-click rate hits 83â93% with AI Overviews / AI Mode. GEO delivers 4.4x higher conversions vs. traditional SEO at $3.71 ROI per dollar.

Operator Signals

James Cadwallader (Profound): Shared Ad Age Tech Power List â positioning Profound in mainstream ad-tech trade press alongside Bose and Reddit.

All other tracked operators quiet overnight.

Voice Highlights

Lily Ray (16h): Reposted Hudgens' GEO book video. Also: YouTube appearance "Lily Ray on AI Slop, GEO, and What Actually Works."

Jason Barnard (5d): "Delegation boundary" â AI pre-screens brands before users see results. Competing for AI confidence, not clicks. Cross-posted to Search Engine Land.

Industry Pulse

[Funding] Scrunch $15M Series A Â· 500+ enterprise brands including Lenovo.

[Research] 94% of digital leaders increasing AEO/GEO spend in 2026 â Conductor CMO Report.

[Recognition] Ad Age Tech Power List coverage signals GEO's arrival in mainstream ad trade media.

item.title: ASO Daily â Monday, May 18, 2026
item.description: Citation Outcome Engineering emerges as the new operating frame for GEO. The category is shifting from generic content activity to verified answer inclusion. Plus: Bluefish closes $43M Series B, Brandlight named CB Insights GEO market leader, HireClix launches JobFlow AEO.
item.content:encoded:
Today's Lead: Citation Outcome Engineering emerges as the new operating frame for GEO

The category is shifting from generic content activity to verified answer inclusion. With 50% of content cited in AI answers being less than 13 weeks old, recency is now a competitive moat â and operators measuring citation outcomes (not impressions) are pulling ahead.

Top Stories

The Death of the Blue Link (National Law Review)
Enterprise legal and compliance teams are flagging GEO as a material disclosure topic. With ChatGPT serving 800M+ weekly users, boards are asking whether brands have a documented strategy for being cited inside AI answers.

60% of U.S. consumers now use generative AI for product research (eMarketer)
Generative AI overtook traditional search for product discovery in three categories: travel, consumer electronics, and SaaS.

Industry Pulse

  • Funding: Bluefish closes $43M Series B. ~10% of the Fortune 500 (Adidas, AmEx, LVMH, Ulta) using it to manage AI visibility.
  • Recognition: Brandlight named GEO market leader by CB Insights, joining Bluefish on the AI 100 list.
  • Product: HireClix launches JobFlow AEO â first vertical AEO play, optimizing job postings to appear in ChatGPT and Perplexity answers.

ASO Daily Â· Vol. 1 Â· Issue No. 1 Â· Curated for Marketing Suite GM Org
This is the inaugural issue. Tomorrow's edition will include overnight LinkedIn posts from the 33 founders and thought leaders Veronica follows.
